args.code:
import json, pandas as pd
path = var_call_0HNmqm4wqN6v5lk90DqStdOT
with open(path,'r',encoding='utf-8') as f:
    recs = json.load(f)
df = pd.DataFrame(recs)
# infer sports by keywords in title/description
text = (df['title'].fillna('') + ' ' + df['description'].fillna('')).str.lower()
kw = r"\b(nfl|nba|mlb|nhl|soccer|football|baseball|basketball|hockey|tennis|golf|olympic|olympics|world cup|cup|championship|playoff|coach|quarterback|touchdown|home run|fifa|uefa|premier league|la liga|serie a|bundesliga|cricket|rugby|nascar|formula 1|f1|grand slam|pga|mls)\b"
mask = text.str.contains(kw, regex=True)
df_s = df[mask].copy()
df_s['desc_len'] = df_s['description'].fillna('').map(len)
row = df_s.sort_values(['desc_len','article_id'], ascending=[False, True]).head(1)
ans = row[['title','article_id','desc_len']].to_dict(orient='records')
print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(ans))

The 6-foot-4 senior, likened by Virginia Coach Al Groh to recent ACC stars Matt Schaub and Philip Rivers, ranks fourth among active NCAA quarterbacks with 9,048 career passing yards. In losses the past two weeks to Penn State and Middle Tennessee, Frye completed 60 of 80 passes for 622 yards. Weather permitting, he will present a challenge for an inexperienced Virginia secondary that made a few too many errors in deep coverage in its first two games."}]

args.code:
import json, pandas as pd
main_path = var_call_0HNmqm4wqN6v5lk90DqStdOT
with open(main_path,'r',encoding='utf-8') as f:
    recs = json.load(f)
df = pd.DataFrame(recs)
df['desc_len']=df['description'].fillna('').map(len)
# choose among long descriptions those likely sports by title containing known indicators
cand = df.sort_values('desc_len', ascending=False).head(500)
kw = r"\b(vs\.?|at\b|no\.|capsules|play host|defeats?|beats?|victory|touchdown|quarterback|coach|season|league|tournament|match|game|final|series|cup)\b"
mask = cand['title'].fillna('').str.lower().str.contains(kw, regex=True)
scand = cand[mask].copy()
# manually also require at least one sports-specific word in title or description
spec = r"\b(football|basketball|baseball|soccer|hockey|tennis|golf|ncaa|nfl|nba|mlb|nhl|cricket|rugby|nascar|f1|olymp)\b"
mask2 = (scand['title'].fillna('').str.lower().str.contains(spec, regex=True)) | (scand['description'].fillna('').str.lower().str.contains(spec, regex=True))
scand2 = scand[mask2]
row = scand2.sort_values(['desc_len','article_id'], ascending=[False, True]).head(1)
ans = row[['title']].to_dict(orient='records')
print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(ans))
